pi_items.source: Task description specifies this path as the location where the report should be created
Chlorine has IDLH of 10 ppm, which is the lowest among the five chemicals (H-Cyanide: 50, Ammonia: 300, CO: 1200, MEK: 3000). Verified via NIOSH database and CDC NIOSH Pocket Guide.
OSHA Permissible Exposure Limit for Hydrogen Cyanide is 10 ppm (TWA). Verified via OSHA chemical data and CDC sources.
NIOSH IDLH for Hydrogen Cyanide is 50 ppm. Verified via CDC NIOSH IDLH database (https://www.cdc.gov/niosh/idlh/74908.html).
OSHA Permissible Exposure Limit for Ammonia is 50 ppm (TWA). Verified via OSHA annotated PEL table and CDC NIOSH Pocket Guide.
NIOSH IDLH for Ammonia is 300 ppm. Verified via CDC NIOSH IDLH database (https://www.cdc.gov/niosh/idlh/7664417.html).
OSHA Permissible Exposure Limit for Carbon Monoxide is 50 ppm (8-hour TWA). Verified via OSHA Annotated Table Z-1 and CDC NIOSH Pocket Guide.
NIOSH IDLH for Carbon Monoxide is 1200 ppm. Verified via CDC NIOSH IDLH database (https://www.cdc.gov/niosh/idlh/630080.html).
OSHA Permissible Exposure Limit for Chlorine is 1 ppm (ceiling limit). Verified via OSHA Annotated Table Z-1 and CDC NIOSH Pocket Guide.
NIOSH IDLH for Chlorine is 10 ppm. Verified via CDC NIOSH IDLH database (https://www.cdc.gov/niosh/idlh/7782505.html) and CDC NIOSH Pocket Guide.
OSHA Permissible Exposure Limit for Methyl Ethyl Ketone is 200 ppm (8-hour TWA). Verified via CDC NIOSH Pocket Guide (https://www.cdc.gov/niosh/npg/npgd0069.html).
NIOSH IDLH for Methyl Ethyl Ketone is 3000 ppm. Verified via CDC NIOSH Pocket Guide and chemical hazard documentation.

privileged_info_summary: All task metadata has been verified against authoritative OSHA and NIOSH sources. The five chemicals and their occupational exposure limits are: Hydrogen Cyanide (PEL: 10 ppm, IDLH: 50 ppm), Ammonia (PEL: 50 ppm, IDLH: 300 ppm), Carbon Monoxide (PEL: 50 ppm, IDLH: 1200 ppm), Chlorine (PEL: 1 ppm ceiling, IDLH: 10 ppm), and Methyl Ethyl Ketone (PEL: 200 ppm, IDLH: 3000 ppm). Chlorine has the lowest IDLH value at 10 ppm, making it the most immediately dangerous among the five chemicals. The report should be created at /home/ga/Documents/exposure_limits_report.txt. All values match official OSHA Permissible Exposure Limits and NIOSH Immediately Dangerous to Life or Health thresholds.
